metadata_information.title: Is the emerging nonfarm market economy the route out of poverty in Vietnam
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: Is the emerging nonfarm market economy the route out of poverty in Vietnam

document_description.abstract: Are the household characteristics that are good for transition to a more diversified market-oriented development process in Vietnam also important for reducing poverty? Or are there tradeoffs? The determinants of both poverty incidence and participation in rural off-farm activities are modeled as functions of household and community characteristics using comprehensive national household surveys for 1993 and 1998. Despite some common causative factors, such as education and region of residence, the processes determining poverty and inhibiting diversification are clearly not the same. Participation in the emerging rural nonfarm market economy will be the route out of poverty for some, but certainly not all, of Vietnam's poor.
